user_title: COO & Global Head of Change Execution, WPS

talking_points.headline: WEF AI Playbook Sets Industry Governance Benchmark — WPS Change Programme Needs a Maturity Assessment
talking_points.why_it_matters: As COO governing the global change execution programme, Surali needs to ensure WPS's AI governance posture meets or exceeds the framework now being used as the benchmark by regulators and client due-diligence processes — gaps will become visible as this standard embeds.
bullets: The World Economic Forum and Accenture published The AI Playbook for Financial Services (24 June), grounded in 18 months of roundtables with 150+ senior leaders from 100+ organisations — this is now the de facto industry reference document for AI governance in financial services

The report identifies governance accountability as the primary differentiator between AI leaders and laggards, mapping a six-dimension maturity framework covering strategy, data, talent, risk, operations, and ethics — regulators in HK, Singapore and the UK have all cited WEF frameworks in recent supervisory guidance

For WPS, the playbook provides a ready-made checklist against which the global change execution programme's AI governance posture should be assessed in Q3, before external mandates formalise these expectations into compliance requirements

source_links.title: WEF: AI Playbook for Financial Services
WEF Press: Financial Institutions Race to Scale AI

talking_points.headline: Swift's 24/7 Blockchain Settlement Is Live — HSBC's Operating Model Must Match Its Infrastructure Lead
talking_points.why_it_matters: HSBC's founding participation in Swift's tokenised payment network is a strategic win, but it creates an immediate operational resilience obligation: risk, compliance, and client servicing functions must be capable of supporting round-the-clock settlement flows before the pilot scales.
bullets: Swift's permissioned blockchain ledger went live 9 July with HSBC as a founding participant among 17 banks; the network enables tokenised deposits to move outside normal banking hours — including nights, weekends and public holidays

This shifts the operating model assumption from 9-to-5 settlement windows to 24/7 availability: operations, risk controls, liquidity management, and client servicing capacity all need to be scoped for continuous operations before the pilot enters production scale

The COO function should lead the cross-functional design for this operating model change — it requires coordination across operations, technology, risk and compliance, making it a natural home in the change execution programme's Q3 agenda

source_links.title: Swift: Blockchain Ledger Ready for Use

talking_points.headline: Wealth Intelligence Live and Asia CIO Seated — Change Execution Velocity Is Now the Measurable Variable
talking_points.why_it_matters: With Wealth Intelligence deployed and the Asia CIO in seat, the programme has moved from change approval to change delivery — the COO's priority should now be establishing the governance cadence and velocity metrics that will define whether the transformation is on track.
bullets: HSBC's Wealth Intelligence generative AI platform is now live across the private bank; incoming head of wealth platforms Nicholas Elias, Head of Wealth Platforms, Wealth & Premier Solutions will scale the platform from deployment to enterprise adoption under his AI expansion mandate

The new Asia CIO for Private Bank and Premier Wealth seated on 6 July, under Willem Sels, Global CIO, HSBC Private Bank and Premier Wealth, completes the decision-making architecture needed to govern AI-driven advisory quality at a regional level

The governance cadence required now: monthly adoption dashboards (RM usage, client touchpoints), a defined Q3 baseline for benchmarking, and a change control process for AI model updates — these are the operational controls that determine whether the investment in Wealth Intelligence converts to measurable business outcomes

source_links.title: HSBC Private Bank: Wealth Intelligence Deployed
Yahoo Finance: HSBC Private Bank Names Asia CIO

AI & Technology.title: WEF AI Playbook for Financial Services: Industry Benchmark for AI Governance Released
AI & Technology.summary: The World Economic Forum, with Accenture, published The AI Playbook for Financial Services at the Annual Meeting of New Champions, drawing on 18 months of roundtables with 150+ senior leaders from 100+ organisations. It identifies governance accountability as the primary differentiator between AI leaders and laggards, and maps a six-dimension maturity framework for enterprise AI deployment.

AI & Technology.title: Swift Blockchain Ledger Goes Live: HSBC, DBS, StanChart Among 17-Bank Tokenised Payment Pilot
AI & Technology.summary: Swift launched its permissioned blockchain ledger on 9 July, enabling 17 banks — including HSBC, Citi, DBS, Standard Chartered, UBS, OCBC and UOB — to pilot 24/7 tokenised cross-border payments outside normal banking hours, built on Ethereum's Linea L2 network developed by ConsenSys.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Deploys Wealth Intelligence AI Platform Across Private Bank
HSBC News.summary: HSBC's Wealth Intelligence generative AI platform is now fully deployed across the private bank, providing relationship managers with personalised market insights and investment strategy recommendations — the operational output of the AI transformation programme led by incoming wealth platforms head Nicholas Elias.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Private Bank and Premier Wealth Appoints New Asia CIO
HSBC News.summary: HSBC appointed a new Asia CIO for Private Bank and Premier Wealth effective 6 July, based in Singapore and reporting to Global CIO Willem Sels and Singapore wealth head Ishan Sarkar — the latest move in HSBC's accelerating build-out of its Asia investment leadership bench.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Bets on AI and $84 Trillion Inheritance Wave to Drive Wealth Boom
HSBC News.summary: HSBC is positioning its wealth business around two structural tailwinds: AI-enabled personalisation at scale and the historic $84 trillion intergenerational wealth transfer wave. The bank plans to use AI to serve mass-affluent clients at near-private-banking quality, deepening its APAC lead while competitors with legacy advisory models face cost and scalability limits.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Asia 2025 AUM League Tables: Assets Cross US$4tn, JP Morgan Enters Top Three Targeting HSBC
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Asia's top 10 private banks grew AUM 22.5% to $2.5 trillion in 2025. JP Morgan Private Bank's 39.1% AUM surge to $299bn puts it within $3bn of HSBC's $302bn regional lead, while Nomura's debut in the rankings signals a broadening competitive field and mounting pressure on HSBC's long-held top position.
